Week 3 Data Visualization Lab with ggplot2

Katherine Quach (A1854014)

January 20, 2026

true 
# Week 3 Data Visualization Lab with ggplot2
## Background There are lots of ways to make figures in R. 
# These include so-called "Base R" graphics (e.g. `plot()`) and 
# tines of add-on packages like ggplot2

# Install the package ggplot2
# install.packages("ggplot2")

##N.B. we never run an install cmd in a quarto code chunk or we
# will end up re-installing packages many many times -
# this is not what we want!

# Any time I want to use this package I need to load it
library(ggplot2)

head(cars)
##   speed dist
## 1     4    2
## 2     4   10
## 3     7    4
## 4     7   22
## 5     8   16
## 6     9   10
# A quick base R plot - this is not ggplot
plot(cars)

library(ggplot2)
ggplot(cars)

# Our first ggplot
# Every ggplot needs at least 3 things: data + aes + geoms
ggplot(data = cars) + 
  aes(x = speed, y = dist) +
  geom_point()

p <- ggplot(data = cars) + 
  aes(x = speed, y = dist) +
  geom_point()
p

# Add a line geom with geom_line
p + geom_line()

# Add a trend line close to the data and render it out
p + geom_smooth()
## `geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'

p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Stopping Distance of Old Cars", subtitles = "Data from Cats Object", x = "Speed (mph)", y = "Distance (ft)")
## Ignoring unknown labels:
## • subtitles : "Data from Cats Object"
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

p + theme_bw()

#--------------------------------------------#

# Read in our drug expression data
url <- "https://bioboot.github.io/bimm143_S20/class-material/up_down_expression.txt"
genes <- read.delim(url)
head(genes)
##         Gene Condition1 Condition2      State
## 1      A4GNT -3.6808610 -3.4401355 unchanging
## 2       AAAS  4.5479580  4.3864126 unchanging
## 3      AASDH  3.7190695  3.4787276 unchanging
## 4       AATF  5.0784720  5.0151916 unchanging
## 5       AATK  0.4711421  0.5598642 unchanging
## 6 AB015752.4 -3.6808610 -3.5921390 unchanging
# Q1. How many genes are in this dataset?
nrow(genes)
## [1] 5196
# Q2. How many columns did you find?
ncol(genes)
## [1] 4
# Q3. How many ‘up’ regulated genes there are?
table(genes$State)
## 
##       down unchanging         up 
##         72       4997        127
# Q4. What fraction of total genes is up-regulated in this dataset?
round((table(genes$State)/nrow(genes))*100, 2)
## 
##       down unchanging         up 
##       1.39      96.17       2.44
# Let's make our first plot attempt
ggplot(data = genes) + aes(x = Condition1, y = Condition2) +
  geom_point()

# Let's color by State to differentiate up and down significant 
# genes compared to unchanging genes
ggplot(data = genes) + 
  aes(x = Condition1, y = Condition2, col = State) +
  geom_point()

# Let's modify the colors to our liking
ggplot(data = genes) + 
  aes(x = Condition1, y = Condition2, col = State) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("blue", "gray", "red")) + 
  labs(title = "Gene Expression Changes Upon GLP-1", 
       x = "Control (no drug)", y = "Drug") + 
  theme_bw()

#--------------------------------------------#

# Find file location online
url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jennybc/gapminder/master/inst/extdata/gapminder.tsv"
gapminder <- read.delim(url)
head(gapminder, 3)
##       country continent year lifeExp      pop gdpPercap
## 1 Afghanistan      Asia 1952  28.801  8425333  779.4453
## 2 Afghanistan      Asia 1957  30.332  9240934  820.8530
## 3 Afghanistan      Asia 1962  31.997 10267083  853.1007
# Install.packages("dplyr")
library(dplyr)
## 
## Attaching package: 'dplyr'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     filter, lag
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union
# Adding more variables to aes()
ggplot(data = gapminder) +
  aes(x=gdpPercap, y=lifeExp, color = continent) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3)

# Let's facet (i.e. making separate plot) by continent rather 
# than what is above
ggplot(data = gapminder) + 
   aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, col = continent) +
   geom_point(alpha=0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~continent)

## Custom Plots
# How big is the gapminder dataset?
nrow(gapminder)
## [1] 1704
ncol(gapminder)
## [1] 6
# I want to filter down a subset of this data so I will use 
# dplyr to help me. First I need to install it and load it up via 
# install.packages("dplyr"), then library("dplyr)
gapminder_2007 <- filter(gapminder, year == 2007)
head(gapminder_2007)
##       country continent year lifeExp      pop  gdpPercap
## 1 Afghanistan      Asia 2007  43.828 31889923   974.5803
## 2     Albania    Europe 2007  76.423  3600523  5937.0295
## 3     Algeria    Africa 2007  72.301 33333216  6223.3675
## 4      Angola    Africa 2007  42.731 12420476  4797.2313
## 5   Argentina  Americas 2007  75.320 40301927 12779.3796
## 6   Australia   Oceania 2007  81.235 20434176 34435.3674
filter(gapminder_2007,country == "Ireland")
##   country continent year lifeExp     pop gdpPercap
## 1 Ireland    Europe 2007  78.885 4109086     40676
filter(gapminder_2007,country == "United States")
##         country continent year lifeExp       pop gdpPercap
## 1 United States  Americas 2007  78.242 301139947  42951.65
# Make a scatterplot comparing 1957 and 2007 for all countries
input <- filter(gapminder, year %in% c(1977, 2007))
ggplot(data = input) +
  aes(x = gdpPercap, y = lifeExp, col = continent, size = pop) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~year)

 #--------------------------------------------#
 
# Bar Charts
 gapminder_top5 <- gapminder %>% 
   filter(year==2007) %>% 
   arrange(desc(pop)) %>% 
   top_n(5, pop)
 
 gapminder_top5
##         country continent year lifeExp        pop gdpPercap
## 1         China      Asia 2007  72.961 1318683096  4959.115
## 2         India      Asia 2007  64.698 1110396331  2452.210
## 3 United States  Americas 2007  78.242  301139947 42951.653
## 4     Indonesia      Asia 2007  70.650  223547000  3540.652
## 5        Brazil  Americas 2007  72.390  190010647  9065.801
# Bar chart showing life expectancy of the five biggest countries by 
# population in 2007 
 ggplot(gapminder_top5) + 
   geom_col(aes(x = country, y = lifeExp, fill = lifeExp))

# Plot population size by country
 q <- ggplot(gapminder_top5) +
   aes(x = reorder(country, -pop), y = pop, fill = country) +
   geom_col(col = "gray30") +
   guides(fill = "none")
 q + labs(title = "GDP per capita and life expectancy", x = "Country", y = "Population")

 # Flipping Bar Charts
 head(USArrests)
##            Murder Assault UrbanPop Rape
## Alabama      13.2     236       58 21.2
## Alaska       10.0     263       48 44.5
## Arizona       8.1     294       80 31.0
## Arkansas      8.8     190       50 19.5
## California    9.0     276       91 40.6
## Colorado      7.9     204       78 38.7
 USArrests$State <- rownames(USArrests)
 ggplot(USArrests) +
   aes(x=reorder(State,Murder), y=Murder) +
   geom_col() +
   coord_flip()

# Making it more tidy
 ggplot(USArrests) +
   aes(x=reorder(State,Murder), y=Murder) +
   geom_point() +
   geom_segment(aes(x=State, 
                    xend=State, 
                    y=0, 
                    yend=Murder), color="blue") +
   coord_flip()
